title: Tapping for This Home, Today
subtitle: An EFT sequence for releasing dissatisfaction with the home you have now
excerpt: An EFT tapping sequence for the persistent dissatisfaction with the home you live in. Works through the comparison, the wish-it-were-different feeling, and the gap between now and the home you picture, toward a grounded place where today's home is worth tending.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s). The stuck-on point draws on common patterns in intentional-living writing around home dissatisfaction.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are caught in comparison between your current home and one you want, and the dissatisfaction is making it hard to be present in the house you actually live in.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the dissatisfaction with the home you have. The script works through the comparison and the wish-it-were-different loop, toward a place where the home you actually live in is good enough to start with today.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I keep comparing this home to the one I picture, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I notice what is wrong with it more than what is good, I am open to releasing that pattern.
Even though the home I want feels far from this one, I accept where I am today.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: This is not the home I imagined for us.
Side of Eye: The gap between what I have and what I picture.
Under Eye: The things I wish were different here.
Under Nose: The frustration of wanting more and not having it yet.
Chin: The feeling that this home is not quite good enough.
Collarbone: The comparison I run without meaning to.
Under Arm: Carrying that dissatisfaction around all the time.
Top of Head: The weight of not quite arriving anywhere.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: This home is where my life is happening right now.
Side of Eye: What I have built here is worth something.
Under Eye: I can want more and still appreciate today.
Under Nose: The home I have is the one I am starting from.
Chin: I can plan for more without dismissing what I have got.
Collarbone: Today's home is allowed to be enough for today.
Under Arm: I can live well here while I work toward more.
Top of Head: This home is real. It is worth tending.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
